questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Cupric compounds are more stable than their cuprous counterparts in solid state. This is because
options: the endothermic character of the 2nd IP of Cu is not so high
size of $\mathrm{Cu}^{2+}$  is less than $\mathrm{Cu}^{+}$
$\mathrm{Cu}^{2+}$ has stabler electronic contiguration as compared to $\mathrm{Cu}^{+}$
the lattice energy released for cupric compounds is much higher than $\mathrm{Cu}^{+}$
questions.solution:
Electronic configurations of cuprous $\left(\mathrm{Cu}^{*}\right)$ and cupric $\left(\mathrm{Cu}^{2}{ }^{+}\right)$ ions are as follows
$\begin{aligned}
\mathrm{Cu}^{+} &=[\mathrm{Ar}] 3 d^{10} 4 s^{\circ} \\
\mathrm{Cu}^{2+} &=[\mathrm{Ar}] 3 d^{9} 4 \mathrm{s}^{\circ}
\end{aligned}$
Thus, electronic configuration of $\mathrm{Cu}^{+}$ is more stable but it is less stable because in $\mathrm{Cu}^{2+}$ due to its small siag the nuclear charge is sufficient to hold 27 electrons but in $Cu^+$ such a condition is not true. Further, the IInd IP of Cu is not very high as compared to its Ist IP. Consequently a large amount of latsicy energy is released for cupric compounds as compared to $Cu^+$ compounds.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: Pyrolusite is $\mathrm{MnO}_2$ used to prepare $\mathrm{KMnO}_4$. Steps are, $\mathrm{MnO}_2 \xrightarrow{\text { I }} \mathrm{MnO}_4{ }^{2-} \xrightarrow{\text { II }} \mathrm{MnO}_4^{-}$. Steps I and II are respectively:
options: fuse with $\mathrm{KOH} /$ air and electrolytic oxidation.
fuse with $\mathrm{KOH} / \mathrm{KNO}_3$ and electrolytic oxidation.
fuse with conc. $\mathrm{HNO}_3$ / air and electrolytic reduction.
dissolve in $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$ and oxidation.

questions.q: A salt $(X)$ is heated in a dry test tube. Reddish brown fumes $(Y)$ are evolved which turn potassium dichromate paper green and starch iodide paper blue $(Y)$ is found to be paramagnetic. Then :
options: X may be $\mathrm{KNO}_3$
X may be $\mathrm{ZnBr}_2$
X may be $\mathrm{Mg}\left(\mathrm{NO}_3\right)_2$
Y turns red litmus blue

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Which of the following statement(s) is (are) correct when a mixture of $\mathrm{NaCl}$ and $\mathrm{K}_{2} \mathrm{Cr}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{7}$ is gently warmed with conc. $\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{SO}_{4} ?$
options: A deep red vapour is evolved
The vapour when passed through NaOH solution, gives a yellow solution
Chiorine gas is also evolved
Chromyl chioride is formed
questions.solution:
(A) When $\mathrm{NaCl}$ and $\mathrm{K}_{2} \mathrm{Cr}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{7}$ warmed with $\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{SO}_{4}$ (i.e. in acidic medium), they produce deep red vapours of chromyl chloride $\left(\mathrm{CrO}_{2} \mathrm{Cl}_{2}\right)$
(B) When NaOH is passod, the product $CrO_{3}$, formed In step (a) will react with NaOH and gives yellow colour solution
(C) Chlorine gas is not evolved, thus false
(D) In the given reaction, chromyl chloride $\left(\mathrm{CrO}_2\mathrm{Cl}_{2}\right)$ is formed. All reactions are as follows
(i) $\mathrm{K}_{2} \mathrm{Cr}_{2} \mathrm{O}_{7}+2 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{SO}_{4} \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{KHSO}_{4}+2 \mathrm{CrO}_{3}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}$
(ii) $\mathrm{NaCl}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{SO}_{4} \longrightarrow \mathrm{NaHSO}_{4}+\mathrm{HCl}$
(iii) $\mathrm{CrO}_{3}+2 \mathrm{HCl} \longrightarrow \mathrm{CrO}_{2} \mathrm{Cl}_{2}$
(iv) $\mathrm{CrO}_{2} \mathrm{Cl}_{2}+2 \mathrm{NaOH} \longrightarrow \mathrm{Na}_{2} \mathrm{CrO}_{4}+2 \mathrm{HCl}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Which of the following statement/statements is/are correct?
options: Solid $\mathrm{I}_2$ is freely soluble in water
Solid $\mathrm{I}_2$ is freely soluble in water but only in presence of excess KI
Solid $\mathrm{I}_2$ is freely soluble in $\mathrm{CCI}_4$
Solid $\mathrm{I}_2$ is freely soluble in hot water
questions.solution:
Hint : $\mathrm{I}_2$ dissolves in aqueous KI to form $\mathrm{KI}_3$.
$\mathrm{I}_2$ also dissolves in $\mathrm{CCl}_4$ as both are non polar compounds.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Which of the following is/are correct?
options: On strong heating $\mathrm{FeCl}_3$ gives $\mathrm{FeCl}_2$ and $\mathrm{Cl}_2$
Dilute $\mathrm{CuCl}_2$ is blue due to formation of $\left[\mathrm{CuCl}_4\right]^{2-}$
$\mathrm{PH}_3$ can be obtained by heating red P with NaOH
$\mathrm{PH}_3, \mathrm{H}_3 \mathrm{PO}_3$ and $\mathrm{H}_3 \mathrm{PO}_2$, all are reducing agents
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{FeCl}_3 \xrightarrow{\Delta} \mathrm{FeCl}_2+\mathrm{Cl}_2$
Dilute $\mathrm{CuCl}_2$ is blue due to $\left[\mathrm{Cu}\left(\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}\right)_4\right]^{2+} . \mathrm{PH}_3$ is obtained be heating white P with NaOH .
All are reducing because of weak $\mathrm{P}-\mathrm{H}$ bond.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
